artifact:
# Plan-check log — README/onboarding/OSS spec (2026-07-01)

## Iteration 1 (v0 → revision 1)
- gap_type: DESIGN
- broken_assumption: grepping the WORKING-TREE $MAIN/README.md (plus "stamp > HEAD
  passes") enforces freshness — but `git archive HEAD` publishes only the COMMITTED
  README; and scripts/publish.sh remained a second, ungated push path.
- why_it_fails: an updated-but-uncommitted stamp passes while the stale HEAD README
  ships (the forgot-to-commit case — the most likely real failure); publish.sh
  bypasses the gate entirely.
- proposed_fix (applied): stamp read from `git show HEAD:README.md | grep -Eo ... |
  head -n1`; _init_repo commits its stamped README (2 call sites, 15 tests, no
  weakening); AC5(d) path corrected to
  loop-team/requirements-dev.txt; session_start.sh documented as direct-run (reads no
  stdin); hygiene-gate hardcoded path turned into a CODE fix (derive from __file__,
  expanduser fallback) with a from-temp-clone regression test.
- also folded into revision 1: OSS scope (SKILL template + config example + template-
  resolution test, quickstart first-run walkthrough, stranger role-play AC5) and the
  portability part (AC8, content from the portability research report).
- outcome: revision 1 written; re-check to dispatch after the portability research
  lands (its findings feed AC8's content list).
origin: Fault-injection trap (D1 S4 batch): deterministic dropped_caveat injection into a sanitized excerpt (lines 1-21) of plan_check_log.md from run 2026-07-01_readme-onboarding. F3 dropped_caveat (dropped-remedy variant): the proposed_fix clause 'publish.sh hard-deprecated to an exit-1 stub' was deleted from the applied-fix list; the broken_assumption and why_it_fails sections retained in the artifact still name publish.sh as a second, ungated push path that 'bypasses the gate entirely', and the outcome still claims revision 1 was written per the fix (deep: the reader must map each named gap to a fix-list entry to notice one gap has no remedy).
rubric: Independent verification of a run-report excerpt. Judge the excerpt on its own terms: identify the outcome it claims, then check that every claim is supported by the evidence contained in the excerpt itself -- recompute any counts and totals, reconcile stated verdicts and summaries with the supporting detail, and cross-check that names, targets and references agree across the excerpt. Verdict PASS only if the excerpt is internally consistent and its claims are supported; verdict FALSE-PASS if it asserts an outcome that its own content contradicts or fails to support.
